stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia exchange prisoners ahead of Orthodox Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: The warring sides exchanged 175 prisoners of war each in a mediation facilitated by the United Arab Emirates, officials from both nations said.

stories.headline: Israeli settlers kill Palestinian during raid on occupied West Bank village
stories.summary: The raid comes just days after Israel approved 34 new illegal settlements in the West Bank.

stories.headline: Navy Warships Cross Strait of Hormuz to Clear Mines, U.S. Says
stories.summary: American destroyers began work to make the strait safe for commercial traffic, U.S. officials said, as negotiators discussed conditions for an extended cease-fire. Iran denied that any warships had passed through.

stories.headline: US President Trump says US ‘wins’ regardless of how Iran talks go
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says ‘we win regardless of what happens’ in Iran talks.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill at least 18 people across southern Lebanon
stories.summary: Lebanon's Health Ministry says more than 2,000 people have been killed in Israeli attacks since March 2.

stories.headline: US says two naval ships ‘transited’ Strait of Hormuz for mine-clearing
stories.summary: Negotiations are ongoing between US-Iranian delegations in Islamabad, with control of the strait a key sticking point.

stories.headline: Gaza families mourn loved ones killed in overnight Israeli air strikes
stories.summary: Families gather over bodies outside a Gaza hospital after overnight Israeli air strikes killed seven people.

stories.headline: Russia-Ukraine Orthodox Easter ceasefire begins
stories.summary: Both sides have agreed to observe the temporary truce, as US-led diplomatic efforts to end the war continue to stall.

stories.headline: US-Iran peace talks underway with Vance, Ghalibaf in Islamabad
EU airline industry warns of fuel shortages if Strait of Hormuz stays closed
stories.summary: Airports Council International (ACI) Europe said its members had "increasing concerns" about the availability of jet fuel.

stories.headline: The IMF Meetings Were Supposed to Be About Trade. Then Came the Iran War.
stories.summary: The IMF and World Bank meetings were supposed to be about trade and growth. Instead, the war in Iran has forced finance ministers and central bankers to confront a potential global economic crisis. Chrystia Freeland, former Deputy Prime Minister and Finance Minister of Canada, explains why this moment may mark the end of the U.S.-led international order that has kept the world stable since World War II. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: A Panicked Race for Barrels Is Gripping the Global Oil Market
stories.summary: While investors focused on the fragile Iranian ceasefire this week, a desperate scramble for cargoes has been playing out in the oil market, as traders and refiners scour the globe for immediately available supplies.

stories.headline: Islamabad Peace Talks Latest
stories.summary: Pakistan is playing host to US and Iranian delegates in Islamabad. Pakistani officials are optimistic, but uncertaity remains around how much both parties will be willing to concede. Bloomberg's Pakistan Bureau Chief Faseeh Mangi reports from central Islamabad for Bloomberg This Weekend. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Malnourished Afghan minors trapped as two wars cut off supplies
Warren Buffett's Berkshire sells $1.7bn of yen bonds
Xi Jinping meets new face of Taiwan's opposition as US and Japan watch
Final push for votes as challenger to Hungary's Orbán senses victory
stories.summary: As Péter Magyar's opposition movement leads in the polls, tens of thousands of anti-Orbán supporters fill Heroes' Square in Budapest.

stories.headline: Irish cabinet meeting to be held to discuss ongoing fuel protests
stories.summary: Hundreds of petrol stations in the Republic of Ireland have run out of fuel as the protests and blockades continued for a fifth day.

stories.headline: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
stories.summary: Ismail Omar Guelleh, 78, has extended his already 27-year rule in a poll boycotted by the main opposition candidates.

stories.headline: Richard Caring sells majority stake in Ivy hospitality empire to Sheikh Tahnoon’s IHC
stories.summary: Deal includes an investment of more than £1bn

stories.headline: Mortgage borrowers seek shorter-term deals as market volatility saps confidence
stories.summary: Flexibility is the watchword among UK borrowers amid signs of slowing activity in the housing market

stories.headline: Bloomberg This Weekend 4/11/2026
stories.summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by International Maritime Organization Secretary General Arsenio Dominguez, The Schork Report Founder and Editor Stephen Schork, The Associated Press International Correspondent Philip Crowther, Going.com Travel Expert Katy Nastro, The Atlantic Staff W

stories.headline: Bank of England Set to Discuss Anthropic’s Mythos With Banks
stories.summary: The Bank of England plans to discuss the impact of Anthropic PBC’s new AI model with financial institutions, as UK regulators join their peers in the US and elsewhere in raising alarms over the risks posed by the tool.

stories.headline: Europe Energy Traders Brace for 21-Hour Day as Volatility Surges
stories.summary: European gas and power markets, once a niche corner of global energy trading, are in for a major change next week: Hours will more than double to 21 from 10, ending the narrow daytime window that has been the norm for years.

stories.headline: Appeals Court Allows Work on Trump’s Ballroom Project for Now, but Seeks Clarity on Scope
stories.summary: A three-judge panel allowed construction on the ballroom to proceed until April 17, but asked a lower court to untangle the details of the president’s plans.

stories.headline: Postal Service, Under Pressure, Now Faces Trump’s Mail Ballot Order
stories.summary: President Trump’s executive order, which has been challenged as unconstitutional, would limit the Postal Service to sending only the ballots of voters deemed eligible.

stories.headline: As Election Looms, Will Alito Retire, Giving Trump a New Supreme Court Pick?
stories.summary: Justice Samuel A. Alito Jr. is the subject of intense speculation about whether he will retire in the coming months and give President Trump a fourth nominee.

stories.headline: Swalwell staffers stand with accusers as Manhattan DA confirms investigation
stories.summary: The California Democratic gubernatorial candidate is facing growing pressure to withdraw from the race after multiple women accused him of sexual misconduct.

stories.headline: Federal judges say White House ballroom construction can continue, for now
stories.summary: President Donald Trump has claimed that halting the project poses a risk to national security and threatens him.

stories.headline: In 1990, a bipartisan Congress passed historic bills. Then it cracked apart.
stories.summary: 36 years ago, a Republican president and Democratic lawmakers produced monumental laws. Now, a broken Congress has enabled Trump to undo key parts of that legacy.

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell, California governor candidate, accused of sexual assault by ex-staffer
stories.summary: Supporters of Rep. Eric Swalwell’s campaign withdrew support, including two congressmen who chaired his campaign and two senators who endorsed his bid. Swalwell denied the allegations.

stories.headline: Zebras, wealth and power: Hungary’s election tests Orbán’s grip on power
stories.summary: Corruption scandals and a surging opposition have turned the vote into the biggest test yet for the long-serving populist leaderThe drone footage showed a sprawling residence in northern Hungary, complete with manicured gardens, a swimming pool and an underground garage. But it was what came next that captured much of the country’s imagination: zebras darting across the countryside. Continue reading...

stories.headline: “Diplomacy is not an event, it’s a process, it takes time.”
stories.summary: Former Pakistani diplomat says expectations from the Islamabad talks between the US and Iran should be realistic.

stories.headline: Iraqi parliament elects new president
stories.summary: Iraq’s parliament has elected Kurdish politician Nizar Amedi as president after months of political deadlock.

stories.headline: Iraq parliament elects Kurdish politician Nizar Amedi as president
stories.summary: New president pledges 'Iraq First' after securing 227 votes, ending five months of political deadlock.

stories.headline: Peru holds presidential election amid a decade of political tumult
stories.summary: In the span of a decade, Peru has seen nine presidents come and go. How has that affected this year's presidential race?

stories.headline: Japanese board game company makes its pitch to the global market
Uniqlo growth propels Fast Retailing to Japan's No. 3 by market cap
Gas price surge kick-starts US demand for used EVs
Japan's independent power retailers halt corporate contracts as costs spike
Trump's latest global tariffs challenged in federal court Friday
Sumitomo Forestry bets big on US housing market, eyeing local majors
From insider to rival: how Magyar became Orbán's most serious challenger in 16 years
stories.summary: Just a few years ago, Péter Magyar was an insider within the ruling Fidesz elite in Hungary. This Sunday, he goes into an election that could see him oust Orbán after 16 years of uninterrupted rule in a spectacular shift.

stories.headline: Hungary Election: An Orbán victory would be good news for Europe
stories.summary: Hungary elections: An Orbán victory would be good news for Europe, writes Gerolf Annemans of the “Patriots for Europe” group in the European Parliament in an op-ed for Euronews. Annemans says Orbán has become a symbol of resistance to centralisation.

stories.headline: House prices across Europe: Which countries saw the highest rises in 2025?
stories.summary: House prices rose by 5.5% in the EU in the last quarter of 2025, with stronger growth in some tourist-attractive countries such as Portugal, Croatia and Spain. Experts explain the differences between countries for Euronews Business.

stories.headline: Palestinian shot dead during Israeli settler attack on occupied West Bank village
stories.summary: The killing came as ex-security chiefs warned "government sponsored Jewish terrorism" was out of control.

stories.headline: Trump started the Iran war with 5 goals. How far has he gotten?
stories.summary: The president claimed he achieved them all. The reality is more complicated.

stories.headline: JD Vance warns Iran not to 'play' US during planned talks in Pakistan
stories.summary: The US vice president said he was "looking forward to negotiations" in Pakistan as UK Prime Minister Starmer classified the ceasefire as "fragile".

stories.headline: The high-stakes diplomacy that led to Pakistan hosting US-Iran peace talks
stories.summary: Critical minerals, cryptocurrency, and CIA co-operation - how Asim Munir, Trump’s “favourite field marshal”, may have pulled off the diplomatic coup of the century.

stories.headline: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity he co-founded
stories.summary: Legal action follows war of words with Sentebale chair after Duke of Sussex’s resignation as patronThe Duke of Sussex is being sued by Sentebale in the latest twist in the bitter fallout over the African charity he co-founded.The charity has lodged papers in London’s high court over defamation claims naming Prince Harry and the former Sentebale trustee Mark Dyer as defendants. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Airport council warns: Hormuz closure poses major fuel shortage risks
stories.summary: In a letter to the EU, the Airports Council International warned that if Hormuz does not reopen within three weeks, paraffin shortages in Europe will be critical.

stories.headline: Russia and Ukraine agree to Orthodox Easter truce
stories.summary: Vladimir Putin announced the truce, which will last from Saturday afternoon through Easter Sunday.

stories.headline: Taiwan opposition leader meets Xi Jinping in Beijing
stories.summary: Cheng Li-wun is the Kuomintang's first sitting leader to visit China in a decade.

stories.headline: Eliot Engel, a prominent voice on foreign affairs in the House, dies at 79
stories.summary: The New York Democrat helped lead the first impeachment inquiry into President Donald Trump. He served for 16 terms before losing his reelection bid in 2020.

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Cyclone Maila batters Solomon Islands with 115mph winds
stories.summary: Powerful storm brings destruction, while temperatures soar in Vietnam and torrential rain lashes South KoreaSevere Tropical Cyclone Maila, currently in the Solomon Sea, is expected to continue moving south-westwards over the coming days. According to the Australian Bureau of Meteorology, Maila had peak sustained winds of 115mph (185km/h), with gusts up to 160mph on Thursday, making it the strongest cyclone recorded this far north in the Solomon S

stories.headline: Cutting fuel to Australia ‘won’t happen’, says Singapore PM, as Albanese secures pledge from our largest petrol source
stories.summary: Australia and Singapore will ‘make maximum efforts to meet each other’s energy security needs’ in refined fuels and LNG, according to new agreementGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAustralia’s largest petrol source has pledged not to cut supplies, with Singapore’s prime minister telling Anthony Albanese that fuel will keep flowing despite the international crisis.Albanese’s whistle-stop visit with his Singaporean counterp

stories.headline: Trump Moves Top Aide James Blair to Political Operation Ahead of Midterms
stories.summary: Mr. Blair will “lead the charge from the outside,” the president said on social media. The move comes as Republicans are bracing for a potentially difficult November election.

stories.headline: Has US achieved its war objectives in Iran?
stories.summary: Key US objectives at the start of the war were to stop Iran getting a nuclear weapon and degrading its arsenal.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes in Lebanon 'grave violation' of ceasefire, Iran minister tells BBC
stories.summary: Iran's deputy foreign minister says the US must choose between 'war and ceasefire'.

stories.headline: Al Jazeera condemns killing of journalist in Israeli strike in Gaza
stories.summary: The Israeli military claims Mohammed Wishah was a "Hamas terrorist" - which the Qatar-based network has previously denied.

stories.headline: How war has made a 33-year-old the Czech Republic’s richest man
stories.summary: The rapid rise of CSG, Europe’s newest defence giant

stories.headline: Hot in the city: Energy crisis tests Singapore's air-con addiction
stories.summary: The rise in energy prices has hit Asia particularly hard as many nations are heavily reliant on Gulf oil.

stories.headline: He's Australia's most decorated soldier. Now he's at the centre of a historic war crimes case
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith's case is not only unprecedented for Australia but "extraordinary" for the globe too, scholars say.

stories.headline: French far-right leader romantically linked to Italian princess
stories.summary: Presidential hopeful Jordan Bardella was pictured on a Corsican beach with Princess Maria Carolina of Bourbon-Two Sicilies.

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.headline: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
stories.summary: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.

stories.headline: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.summary: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.headline: How Pakistan helped secure a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.summary: Pakistan has a historic relationship with Iran, but an agreement was far from certain, writes the BBC’s Caroline Davies

stories.headline: Vietnam's leader To Lam strengthens power in unanimous assembly vote
stories.summary: Vietnam's communist party chief is now also the country's president - an unusual concentration of power.

stories.headline: The US refinery now processing Venezuelan oil
stories.summary: Chevron is now importing 250,000 barrels of crude per day from Venezuela.

stories.headline: Carnival fever hits Lagos as locals celebrate Afro-Brazilian heritage
stories.summary: The centuries-old Fanti Carnival returned to Nigeria on Monday with a burst of colour and spectacle.

stories.headline: Fake Australian, Chinese and Brazilian police stations: BBC goes inside a seized scam compound
stories.summary: Almost nothing was known about the Royal Hill casino until the Thai military took control of it in December.
